title: Salesforce Data Export and Report Download Spikes
dataSources: `index=salesforce` (or tenant-specific routed index) from the Splunk Add-on for Salesforce: **`salesforce:event_log`** ingests Salesforce **Event Log File (ELF)** JSON via the **Event Log File REST API**, **polled hourly by default (interval=3600s)** unless your TA configuration lowers it—**requires Salesforce Event Monitoring add-on and/or Salesforce Shield** entitlements because ELF retrieval is licensed. For this UC emphasize **`EventType` values** **`ReportExport`**, **`DataExport`**, and **`BulkApiResult`**: routinely inspect **`RowsProcessed`** (may be absent on some exports—pair with **`eval`** fallbacks), **`EntityName`** (object/report context), **`UserId`** / **`Username`**, **`ApiType`**, timestamps, and request correlation IDs when surfaced. Optionally augment with **`salesforce:login_history`** later for analyst drilldown—not required for primary detection. **Data flow:** Salesforce → OAuth REST collection (HF recommended) → parsed events → indexer. **CIM note:** **No Splunk CIM mapping** ships for Salesforce security events; payloads remain **vendor-proprietary JSON**—build steward-approved lookups for regulated object lists instead of forcing CIM tags.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)), ≥5.0. Install on the Search Head (for field extractions and saved searches) and on a dedicated Heavy Forwarder if running data collection off-SH. The TA provides both collection and parsing. The TA requires a Salesforce Connected App with OAuth 2.0 credentials and an Event Monitoring or Salesforce Shield license.
spl:
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType IN ("ReportExport","DataExport","BulkApiResult")
| stats count as exports, dc(EntityName) as unique_objects, sum(RowsProcessed) as total_rows by UserId, Username
| where exports > 10 OR total_rows > 50000
| sort -total_rows
description: Detects unusually heavy report exports, data export jobs, and bulk query result events by counting operations and summed row processing per user—highlighting abnormal pull patterns consistent with stealthy data harvesting.
value: Large-scale downloads underpin regulatory breaches, PCI and privacy fines, litigation, and customer churn; spotting outliers fast preserves evidence chains for compliance exams and proves your team actively monitors custodial datasets.
implementation: Enable detailed event log ingestion for export-related EventTypes; map RowsProcessed where available; align thresholds with legitimate analyst and revops workloads; enrich with HR role lookups for insider context.
visualization: **Row 1 —** Single value trio: distinct users over row threshold, cumulative **`RowsProcessed`**, peak export jobs/hour (**time picker default Last 24 hours**); red KPI when any single user breaches both `exports` and `total_rows` SLAs. **Row 2 —** Heatmap (**x** = hour-of-day, **y** = weekday) of export counts for **service vs human** personas (split by `Username` regex / lookup). **Row 3 —** Sortable detail table: **`Username`, `exports`, `unique_objects`, `total_rows`, dominant `EntityName`**, last successful login country (joined). **Row 4 —** Stacked column timechart (**`span=4h`**) comparing **`ReportExport`** vs **`DataExport`** vs **`BulkApiResult`** volumes with drilldown opening raw ELF JSON sidebar.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce — Data Collection Configuration

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Quarterly regulatory filings & SEC/FINRA/insurance reporting:** GRC teams export wide fact tables for filings that legitimately exceed static `exports`/`total_rows` thresholds for a few days—**mitigate** with `finance_reporting_windows` lookups (ROE + approved ticket), correlate `index=changes` or ServiceNow change tasks, and downgrade to Slack while still logging to a summary index for audit. (b) **Audited warehouse/ lakehouse refreshes:** Fivetran, MuleSoft, or native replication jobs raise **BulkApiResult** volumes overnight—**mitigate** using `data_platform_integration` allowlists keyed on `UserId`/`ClientId`, dynamic baselines per `EntityName` family, and 30-day warmup after new pipelines. (c) **Authorized Tableau/Einstein/CRM Analytics prep:** analysts run repeat **ReportExport** bursts before board meetings—**mitigate** join corporate VPN/Zscaler egress, require `business_justification` in a lightweight intake form mirrored to Splunk lookup nightly. (d) **Post-campaign marketing pulls:** RevOps and growth teams ship large lead CSVs after major campaigns—**mitigate** HR title/department tags, campaign calendar overlays, and temporary sigma wideners with expiry dates. (e) **Migration cut-overs & backfills:** legacy-to-Salesforce conversions create short, intense export waves—**mitigate** PMO-published `cutover_moratorium` macros, dual approval on suppressions, and segregated non-prod indexes. (f) **Sandbox full-copy / training exports:** developers dump masked copies for ML feature stores—**mitigate** strict `OrganizationId!=production` routing, HF collection split, and quarterly review of stale allowlists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **`Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ≥5.0** on **Search Heads** (field extractions, saved searches, dashboards) and, for production hygiene, a **Heavy Forwarder** that performs OAuth-authenticated REST collection away from interactive admin sessions.
• **Salesforce entitlements & app security:** **Event Monitoring add-on** and/or **Salesforce Shield** for **Event Log File (ELF) REST** access; **Connected App** with **OAuth 2.0**; least-privilege integration user; credentials in Splunk **storage/passwords** or your secret manager—not cleartext in `local/inputs.conf`.
• **Indexes & RBAC:** route to **`index=salesforce`** (or `salesforce_prod` vs `salesforce_sb` macros); SOC and IAM analysts get **`srchIndexesAllowed`** without `admin`; restrict dashboard tokens exposing `Username`/`UserId` broadly.
• **Ingest economics:** default ELF poll **3600s**; pilot 7–14 days and forecast **GB/day**; trim unnecessary EventTypes if license pressure emerges; document `_indextime` vs `_time` skew monitoring for catch-up bursts.
• **Lookups & governance:** provision change-controlled CSV/KV lookups (allowlists, object classification tiers, integration registries); define **RACI** across CRM platform engineering, SOC, IAM, Data Owners, and Legal for escalations touching regulated datasets.
• **Extraction regression guard:** after each TA upgrade, run `fieldsummary` on the UC’s canonical fields for 10k recent events and archive results in Git to catch JSON path drift early.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable the `salesforce:event_log` input (and login feeds if correlated later). Poll interval default 3600 seconds. Key fields: UserId, Username, EventType, EntityName, RowsProcessed, ApiType, timestamps. Confirm `ReportExport`, `DataExport`, and `BulkApiResult` events populate; normalize null RowsProcessed with `fillnull` upstream if dashboards require totals.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Schedule during business and off-hours cadences to catch batch abuse:

```spl
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType IN ("ReportExport","DataExport","BulkApiResult")
| stats count as exports, dc(EntityName) as unique_objects, sum(RowsProcessed) as total_rows by UserId, Username
| where exports > 10 OR total_rows > 50000
| sort -total_rows
```

Understanding this SPL
The pipeline filters to export-class events, aggregates counts, distinct sensitive objects touched, and cumulative rows processed per identity, then applies dual thresholds to catch both chatter and massive one-off pulls. Tune `exports` and `total_rows` to your firm’s baselines; consider splitting financial vs. service cloud personas via lookup.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare hits to Salesforce report history, Data Loader jobs, and integration user service accounts. Interview report owners for legitimate bulk work; capture allowlisted service principals in a KV store to suppress recurrence while preserving visibility.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Pair with HR termination dates and privileged access reviews, push high-severity rows into SecOps queues, and trend month-over-month download volume on an executive summary panel to show control effectiveness to risk committees.

Exports, downloads, and insider data harvesting — extended implementation economics
Treat **ReportExport**, **DataExport**, and **BulkApiResult** as a family of egress behaviors differentiated by Salesforce mechanics but unified in insider-risk narrative. Institutional controls often require **dual thresholds** (`exports > N` AND `RowsProcessed sum > M`) precisely because crafty actors may attempt many moderate-sized pulls rather than one massive pull—your detection should degrade gracefully when `RowsProcessed` is null by folding in **`EntityName` cardinality** and **distinct object families** surfaced through a lookup mapping objects to confidentiality tiers.

Data classification overlay
Maintain `sf_object_classification.csv` listing `EntityName` → `{tier,regulated_flag,pci_scope}`. Append `tier` prior to alerting and escalate automatically when **`tier` is one of `restricted`, `pci`, or `phi`** even when raw counts appear borderline. This aligns Splunk alerting with enterprise **taxonomy** committees and prevents generic thresholds from under-protecting the most harmful datasets.

Architectural safeguards beyond Splunk
Where possible, implement **Salesforce Shield** classification, **Transaction Security Policies**, **Event Monitoring realtime** (**if licensed**), and **E-Discovery** tooling as upstream guardrails Splunk corroborates rather than replaces. Splunk excels at **historical trending** and **cross-system corroboration** (DLP egress, proxy uploads, insider HR flags), forming a layered story regulators appreciate.

Operational triage playbook expansion
Tier-1 verifies **account authenticity** (`Username` casing drift, dormant account resurrection). Tier-2 validates **authorization** via **delegated administrator** scopes and confirms whether export occurred through **Enhanced Domains**, **Experience Cloud**, or **API**. Tier-3 coordinates **temporary suspension** decisions with Legal for employee subjects. Always snapshot **permission summary** Salesforce reports (procedure documented, not Splunk-hosted) contemporaneously.

SPL alternatives and refinements
Add `fillnull RowsProcessed value=0` only after confirming null means zero rather than absent telemetry—misapplication under-estimates severity. Split searches: one for **interactive users** excluding known integration prefixes, another invert for **service accounts** with separate thresholds keyed by **`sf_integration_risk`** lookup. Introduce **`streamstats`** to identify **sustained** export bursts across multiple contiguous hours versus single anomalies.

Correlation with endpoint and egress telemetry
Join `Username`/`email` to **UEBA** summaries or **CASB/DLP** events where large CSV creation or cloud upload aligns temporally (**±30m**). This reduces false negatives when attackers **stage** extracts locally prior to offsite transfer. Respect privacy minimization directives when joining datasets across jurisdictions.

Validation using business calendars
Establish **golden weeks** devoid of filings or campaigns quarterly; during those intervals, elevations should approach zero—if not, thresholds are mis-tuned.

Retention and forensic readiness
Align index retention so that **six months** minimum of export-class events remain online for AML/fraud retrospective investigations. For lawsuits, educate legal on **immutable storage** alternatives or **cold S3 archiving** pipelines outside Splunk if required.

Incident metrics and learning loops
Capture **average rows per export**, **median**, and **spread** metrics weekly; sudden increases in dispersion often precede **tooling swaps** such as migrating from legacy ODBC to Bulk API parallel jobs—pre-notify SOC when platform engineering announces such changes.

Runbook escalation matrix
Severity 1 (critical): **`pci`/`phi` tiers** impacted or >250k rows. Severity 2: broad object diversity with regulated mix. Severity 3: threshold-only breach confined to **`public marketing`** classifications. Maintain RACI aligning CRM platform owner, SOC, Fraud, IAM, Legal.

Purple team scenario — synthetic export storm
Coordinate with admins to simulate a controlled export storm in QA; ingest into QA index verifying alert logic before production policy changes roll out.

Search performance partitioning
Separate **Financial Services Cloud** versus **standard CRM** workloads by `OrganizationType`/`BusinessUnitId` macros if multitenant ingestion combines streams—prevent cross-noise thresholds.

Quarterly KPI set
Median rows per user per week, regulated-object hit rate, recurrence by department, SLA adherence. Present to Operational Risk forum.

Quarterly KPI set (continued)
Trend **silent hours** anomalies (activity when exports historically zero). Track correlation success rate between Splunk alerts and final case dispositions adjudicated insider vs benign.

Backup and transactional corroboration
Where corporate backup tooling captures SFDC nightly snapshots, **do not rely** on Splunk for restore execution—Splunk informs **prioritization** of which tables and time windows warrant deeper backup diff analysis.

Executive summary scaffolding
Maintain three templates: insider theft attempt, benign integration drift, sanctioned compliance extraction—swap numbers and identities programmatically via tokens for leadership briefings.

Disaster recovery & collector considerations
Mirrors UC-10.12.46: gap detection plus manual validation of resumed exports counts to avoid phantom spikes.

Evidence packaging standard
Screenshots discouraged; prefer **immutable Splunk dashboards** exported as signed PDF snapshots with SPL appendix.

Continuous improvement backlog
Harvest analyst feedback tags monthly; prioritize template adjustments.

Third-party SaaS aggregator risk
Exports via **analytics hubs** legitimately multiplex—encode vendor registry with expected row bands.

Adaptive threshold research
Pilot **STL decomposition** offline for flagship objects; migrate only after stats review.

Collaboration etiquette
Quarterly luncheon walkthrough fosters trust between RevOps & SOC.

Psychological safety
Celebrate false positives that surfaced broken monitoring assumptions.

FAQ for auditors
Explain Event Monitoring entitlement path, segregation of duties for threshold edits, and dual control on suppressions.

Field normalization caveats
`Username` merges must respect **federated identity mapping** quirks.

Holiday calendar integration
Regional bank holidays suppressed via lookup to avoid needless noise.

Synthetic monitoring hook
Synthetic admin check not recommended for exports—risk of real data motion.

Post-incident retrospective template
Timeline, impacted records estimate, controls reinforced.

Metric guardrails
Cap max search time range per saved search to control cost.

Role-based training microlearning
10-minute modules for RevOps export champions.

Exit criteria for tuning tickets
Document sign-off from Data Owner + SOC lead.

Cloud provider transparency
If using **Splunk Cloud**, document data residency for CRM telemetry.

API version skew
When Salesforce API versions shift, revalidate field names in sample JSON weekly during migration window.

Insider legal review cadence
Annual refresh of monitoring charter.

Customer trust angle
Optional customer notification playbooks if exports involve end-customer PII at scale.

Board-level metric
Annual count of **critical** export alerts with confirmed malicious or policy-violating disposition (should be near zero with strong controls).

Governance, evidence, and continuous control testing
Establish this detection as a named IT General Control for custodial CRM data. Each quarter, export a statistically significant sample of alerts (for example, 25 cases) and perform a **quality review** covering five dimensions: (1) timeliness relative to Salesforce poll latency, (2) accuracy versus ground truth in Salesforce admin screens, (3) completeness of ticketing metadata, (4) appropriateness of suppressions, and (5) analyst narrative quality suitable for regulatory interviews. Store evidence in your GRC repository with hashes and retention aligned to your legal hold policy. When Splunk or the Salesforce TA upgrades, repeat a **regression harness** that replays frozen JSON fixtures through a dev indexer to ensure field extractions remain stable. Maintain a **change advisory** record whenever thresholds, lookup schemas, or cron schedules change, because auditors will ask for a traceable history of tuning decisions.

Performance, workload, and search optimization
Salesforce security searches can become expensive if they scan wide time ranges without partition discipline. Prefer **index-time routing** that isolates production org data, and use **`tstats` only when you have a compatible data model**—which is rarely true for raw Salesforce JSON—so focus on **fast filters** (`sourcetype`, `EventType`, `index`) and **summary indexing** for baselines. On Splunk Cloud, monitor **search budget** consumption for the service account running these schedules; split heavy baseline jobs from alert jobs. For on-premises, place collection on a **Heavy Forwarder** to keep REST polling off search heads. If you enable **workload management**, assign these searches to a pool with guardrails so ad-hoc human searches do not starve operational detections.

Risk communication and executive narrative
Translate technical hits into business language. For each incident class, maintain a one-paragraph **executive summary template** describing customer impact, financial exposure, and remediation status. This elevates the UC from a SOC tool to a board-ready control narrative, especially in financial services where CRM data includes wealth, insurance, or mortgage artifacts. Pair metrics with **leading indicators** (integration onboarding rate, active connected apps) so risk committees can ask forward-looking questions rather than only reviewing rear-view alert counts.

Deep-dive operational walkthrough — export-class detections in production
Day zero through day thirty of this control should be treated as a **calibration epoch**, not an enforcement epoch. For the first two weeks, run the SPL in **report mode** with broadened thresholds (for example, double the `exports` and `total_rows` gates) and tag results as **calibration-only** using a Splunk eval flag. Hold daily 15-minute stand-ups with CRM platform engineering and RevOps leadership to review the top ten noisy users and decide which should be allowlisted with expirations. Document every allowlist decision with a **Data Owner attestation** reference because regulators will ask why certain exports no longer page the SOC.

During weeks three and four, tighten thresholds incrementally while monitoring **alert precision** (true positives / (true positives + false positives)) weekly. If precision falls below 40%, pause tightening and invest in **lookup enrichment** rather than more aggressive math. Enrichment examples include joining `Username` to an HR feed for **department** and **job code**, joining `ClientId` (if present on parallel API logs) to integration ownership, and tagging **Experience Cloud** guest users differently from internal employees.

From day thirty onward, transition to full enforcement with **tiered severities**. The alert action should include a **structured JSON payload** consumed by your SOAR platform when available. Minimum fields: `alert_id`, `UserId`, `Username`, `exports`, `unique_objects`, `total_rows`, `top_entities`, `event_types_breakdown`, and `splunk_view_url`. If SOAR is not available, enforce a **minimum ticket template** with the same semantics in ServiceNow fields.

Operational shift handover discipline matters for export detections because they often correlate with pressing business timelines (quarter-end, campaign launches). Encourage SOC analysts to annotate whether the alert aligns with known business rhythms using a normalized disposition vocabulary: `BENIGN_KNOWN_CAMPAIGN`, `BENIGN_INTEGRATION`, `INVESTIGATE_INSIDER`, `INVESTIGATE_ACCOUNT_TAKEOVER`, `ESCALATE_LEGAL_HOLD_CONFLICT`.

Create a recurring **Splunk anomaly review** dashboard showing the trailing 90-day precision metrics and backlog age for open investigations tied to exports. Operational risk committees appreciate seeing backlog age because stale investigations undermine the control’s credibility during audits even if detections technically fire correctly.

Technical validation during calibration should include reproducibility drills: pick an alert from week one, rerun the SPL over the same `_time` window after TA upgrades mid-quarter, and confirm identical tuple outcomes modulo expected field renaming. Capture outcomes in Git-managed runbooks referencing Splunk TA version hashes.

Quarterly retrospective topics should cover: emergence of **new Salesforce features** altering export telemetry (Bulk API v2 parallelism, Enhanced Domains quirks), changes in sanctioned analytics tooling, shifts in offshore business processing centers that export large regional datasets nightly, and any major **privacy regulation** changes affecting lawful monitoring scope.

Finally, emphasize **human factors**: export alerts can unfairly spotlight power users in revenue teams. Train analysts to employ neutral language in tickets, avoid premature accusations, and route sensitive findings through HR/Legal channels per policy. The goal is to protect customers and the firm, not to create internal adversarial culture.
Schedule as Alert: cron `15 * * * *` (offset from top-of-hour ELF landings), earliest/latest **`-70m@m` to `-10m@m`** to avoid incomplete trailing buckets, alert when **number of results > 0**, **throttle** on `UserId` for **6 hours** (exports may repeat), **suppress** known `Username` patterns from `sf_export_allowlist` with expirations, adaptive severity: **severity=5** if regulated object tier from lookup else **severity=3**.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Symptom — Alert never fires despite obvious exports in Salesforce UI** — likely wrong `EventType` labels for your TA version, events landing in sandbox index only, or `RowsProcessed` null causing `total_rows` under-threshold silently; fix by validating `| stats values(EventType)` over 24h and temporarily lowering thresholds in dev.  
• **Symptom — Massive spike only at ingestion catch-up after collector outage** — `_indextime` clusters while `_time` spans days; mitigate with catch-up mute macro or conditional `where _indextime-_time < 7200`.  
• **Symptom — RowsProcessed double-counts** — duplicate modular inputs poll same ELF intervals; dedup `source`/`offset` fields if exposed or tighten to single HF collector.  
• **Symptom — Service accounts overwhelm table** — expected; pivot human vs integration via regex/`lookup sf_integration_accounts` before paging.  
• **Symptom — Regex object tier lookup misses new custom object** — refresh `sf_object_classification.csv` weekly and add stewardship review ticket.  
• **Symptom — Performance timeouts** — replace broad `*` with explicit index + sourcetype macros, shorten window, summary-index hourly aggregates as documented earlier.  
• **Symptom — Legal argues monitoring over-broad** — provide data minimization packet: fields retained list, SOC role segregation, DPIA linkage.
grandmaExplanation: We notice when someone downloads far more spreadsheets and customer lists than normal, because walking out with giant piles of information is often how insiders or stolen accounts sneak data away before anyone realizes it is gone.
